compliance_rule: Every GAQP-compliant claim record must carry all required fields. A claim missing any required field is not GAQP-compliant. A claim record that exists in a .intel file is by definition admitted — there are no rejected or needs_review records in a compliant .intel file.

fields.definition: Sequential serial handle assigned at extraction time. Unique within the .intel package.
SHA-256 deterministic identity hash of claim_text + source_anchor_id. The true unique identifier. Two records with the same fingerprint are the same claim.
Discriminator for unified corpus stores. Always 'claim' for nugget records.
ISO 8601 timestamp of when this nugget was born. When the .intel file was generated.
The governed claim — the atomic nugget. Exact, governed wording as extracted and admitted.
One of the exact 25 canonical types from the closed claim register. No substitution, abbreviation, or inference permitted.
Verbatim passage from the source document from which this claim was extracted. The raw input before governed extraction.
TRUE if the claim involves any deconstructor inference beyond what the source explicitly states. Inference must be labeled — never hidden.
Foreign key to the source record in the source manifest. Machine-readable reference.
Human-readable name of the source document. For display and citation without requiring a join.
SHA-256 hash of the source file at time of extraction. Enables stale detection — if source_hash no longer matches the source file, the nugget is stale.
Foreign key to the anchor record. The anchor carries precise location data (character offsets, quote hash, etc.).
Page number within the source document. Human-readable location. Required for the nugget to stand alone without an anchor lookup. NULL for non-paginated sources.
Section heading or label within the source document. Human-readable location.
Paragraph number within the source section. Human-readable location.
Single dominant sector from the controlled sector vocabulary. NULL for sector-agnostic nuggets. NULL is valid and common — not an error.
Pipe-delimited sector:subtag pairs from the domain modules taxonomy. Format: SectorCode:SubtagCode. Example: HC:MOB|HC:Medicare|RE:MOB. NULL for sector-agnostic nuggets.

Pipe-delimited cross-cutting tags using controlled prefixes. Format: prefix:Value. Prefixes: func (business function), risk (risk category), topic (cross-sector subject), geo (geography). Example: func:Procurement|risk:VendorConcentration|geo:APAC. NULL is valid.
Pipe-delimited named entities referenced in the claim. Organizations, persons, products, programs, regulations.
Pipe-delimited fingerprints of related nuggets within the same .intel package. Non-authoritative hints for reconstruction graph construction.
Confidence level from the canonical ladder.
Numeric score per confidence ladder. seed=0.50, single_source=0.65, developing=0.72, corroborated=0.91, structural=1.00, disputed=null.
Lifecycle status of the nugget. active = current and valid. stale = source_hash mismatch detected, regeneration needed.
Pipe-delimited validation flags raised during or after extraction. NULL means no flags.
How the claim was produced.
Identifier or fingerprint of the instruction set used to govern this deconstruction. Records the art of the extraction without exposing proprietary prompt content.
Version of the GAQP Standards Package (the schematic) that governed this extraction. Must be recorded on every artifact.
Operator or LLM annotation about the nugget itself — unusual source context, ambiguous classification, notable inference. Not about admission status.
Post-admission operator notes added during corpus review or corroboration.

Analyst-applied override to the default recency decay class derived from claim_type. NULL means use the claim-type default from confidence_modifiers.json. Non-null means the extracting analyst has judged that this specific claim has atypical temporal stability relative to its type default. Example: a Doctrine claim that functions as a timeless operational principle receives 'timeless' regardless of the default fast_decay class assigned to Doctrine. Required when inference_flag is TRUE and the inferential distance affects temporal stability.
fields.adoption_note: Added as field 30 per IQS Standards Committee adoption 2026-05-25. Elevated from proposed to constitutional based on independent architectural review confirming the override mechanism is necessary to handle intra-type temporal variance, particularly for Doctrine in fast-moving domains.

removed_fields.reason: Existence in .intel file = admitted. No rejected records stored.
No rejected claims — they are not born, not recorded.
Same. Merged concept into review_notes for post-admission use.
Seven test fields removed. Nugget existence is proof of passing all seven.
Redundant — first value in tags_pipe or domain_tags_pipe by convention.
Anchor territory. Carried in source_anchor record, not nugget.
Replaced by domain_tags_pipe which encodes sector implicitly.
Domain_tags_pipe provides superior granularity. SIC crossrefs on sector_vocabulary record.
